plan.summary: Implement a dark mode feature for the website, allowing users to toggle between light and dark themes, with preference persistence and system theme detection.

steps.description: Define CSS Custom Properties (Variables) for Theming
steps.details: Modify the main CSS file (e.g., `style.css`) to define CSS custom properties for colors, backgrounds, borders, and other stylistic elements. Define a default set of variables for the light theme under `:root` and override these variables for the dark theme under a specific class, such as `body.dark-mode`.

steps.description: Create a Theme Toggle UI Element
steps.details: Add an HTML element (e.g., a button, switch, or icon) to a prominent location in your `index.html` or main template file (e.g., header, footer) that users can click to toggle the theme. Assign it a unique ID for JavaScript interaction.

steps.description: Implement JavaScript Logic for Theme Switching and Persistence
steps.details:
Add JavaScript code to your main script file (e.g., `script.js`) to handle the theme switching logic. This includes: 
1. Checking for a saved theme preference in `localStorage` on page load. 
2. Detecting the user's system preference for dark mode (`prefers-color-scheme`). 
3. Applying the appropriate theme class (e.g., `dark-mode`) to the `<body>` element. 
4. Adding an event listener to the theme toggle UI element to switch the theme class on the `<body>` and save the new preference to `localStorage`.

steps.description: Update Existing CSS to Utilize Theme Variables
steps.details: Review all existing CSS rules and replace hardcoded color values (e.g., `#333`, `white`) with the newly defined CSS custom properties (e.g., `var(--text-color)`, `var(--background-color)`). Ensure all UI components, text, links, buttons, and backgrounds adapt correctly to both light and dark themes.

Thoroughly test the dark mode functionality across various web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and devices (desktop, tablet, mobile). Verify that the theme toggle works, the theme persists across sessions, and all elements are styled correctly with adequate contrast in both light and dark modes. Check for any visual glitches or 'flickering' during theme changes.
Once testing is complete and all issues are resolved, deploy the updated HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files to the production server.

risks: Incomplete styling of all website elements in dark mode, leading to inconsistent UI or unreadable text.
Poor color contrast in dark mode, causing accessibility issues for users with visual impairments.
Flickering or sudden theme changes on page load if JavaScript is slow or not optimized.
Performance impact if CSS variables are overused or not managed efficiently on very large sites.
plan.rollback_strategy: Revert the `index.html`, `css/style.css`, and `js/script.js` files to their previous versions from version control. Clear `localStorage` in browsers for affected users if necessary.
plan.success_criteria: The website successfully displays in both light and dark modes. Users can toggle between themes using the provided UI element. The chosen theme persists across browser sessions. All website elements are visually consistent and readable in both themes, meeting accessibility standards for color contrast.
plan.original_request: Add dark mode to my website

user_request: Add dark mode to my website
